description:
To properly set the permissions of /var/log/localmessages, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0644 /var/log/localmessages
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/localmessages</tt> file contains log messages from certain boot scripts,
including the DHCP client, and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /var/log/localmessages, run the command: 
$ ls -l /var/log/localmessages
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rw-r--r--
fixtext:
Configure the "/var/log/localmessages" file to "0644" by running the following command:

$ sudo chmod 0644 /var/log/localmessages
title: Verify Permissions on /var/log/localmessages(.*) Files
